inequantiles 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Introduction to inequantiles: Quantile-Based Inequality Measures for Survey Data

CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

Project Overview

inequantiles is an R package for estimating quantile-based inequality indicators (QRI, QSR, Palma ratio, percentile ratios) from survey microdata, with full support for complex sampling designs and weighted quantile estimation.

Publication Goals

The publication roadmap is, in order:

  1. CRAN submission — must happen first; the R Journal requires the package to be on CRAN or Bioconductor before a paper can be accepted.
  2. R Journal paper — a methods article describing the package, to be submitted after CRAN acceptance.

CRAN Checklist (reference: https://r-pkgs.org/release.html)

  • Every exported function has @returns and @examples in its roxygen2 docs
  • No \dontrun{} — use @examplesIf for examples with optional dependencies
  • Authors@R includes a copyright holder (role = "cph")
  • URL and BugReports fields present in DESCRIPTION
  • Version bumped to a proper release (e.g. 0.1.0) via usethis::use_version()
  • cran-comments.md created via usethis::use_cran_comments()
  • devtools::check(remote = TRUE, manual = TRUE) — 0 errors, 0 warnings, NOTEs explained
  • devtools::check_win_devel() passes (tests against R-devel on Windows)
  • urlchecker::url_check() — all URLs valid
  • A testthat test suite exists under tests/
  • Submit via devtools::submit_cran(); click the confirmation link in CRAN’s email

R Journal Checklist (reference: https://journal.r-project.org/)

  • Article written with rjtools (rjtools::create_article()), producing both HTML and PDF
  • Maximum 20 pages; abstract ≤ 250 words in plain text
  • Cover letter explaining suitability
  • _Rpackages.txt listing all packages used (one per line)
  • Fully reproducible: all code/data publicly available; full run completes in < 10 minutes
  • Figures and tables include alt-text
  • Run rjtools::initial_check_article() and resolve all issues before submitting
  • Submit as a ZIP (< 10 MB) via the official Google Form on the journal website

Common Commands

All development uses the standard R devtools workflow (run in an R console):

devtools::load_all()      # Load package for interactive testing
devtools::document()      # Regenerate docs from roxygen2 comments (run after editing @param/@return)
devtools::check()         # Full CRAN compliance check (build + tests + examples)
devtools::build()         # Build source tarball
devtools::install()       # Install locally

Run a single example/test:

devtools::load_all()
example(qri)              # Run examples from a specific function's documentation

Build vignettes:

devtools::build_vignettes()

Architecture

Core Computation Layer

R/csquantile.R — the foundation of the entire package. All inequality measures delegate to csquantile() for quantile estimation. It supports: - Types 4–9 (Hyndman-Fan methods extended for weighted data): cumulative weight calculations with type-specific adjustments to (h, j) interpolation parameters - Harrell-Davis (HD): uses the incomplete beta function (pbeta) as weights over the empirical distribution - Handles NULL weights (falls back to unweighted), boundary cases (p=0, p≈1), and na.rm

Inequality Measures (all in R/)

Each measure follows the same pattern — call csquantile() at specific probability points, then apply the measure’s formula:

File Measure Formula summary
qri.R Quantile Ratio Index Average of 100 ratios R(p) = Q(p/2) / Q(1−p/2) over p ∈ (0,1)
qsr.R Quintile Share Ratio Mean income of top 20% / mean income of bottom 20%
palma_ratio.R Palma index Q(0.90+) share / Q(0.40−) share
ratio_quantiles.R Percentile ratios e.g., Q(0.90) / Q(0.10)

Grouped Data Layer (R/*_grouped.R)

Separate functions (quantile_grouped, qri_grouped, gini_grouped) handle pre-aggregated/binned data (e.g., fiscal tabulations) via linear interpolation within class intervals. These handle open-ended first/last classes (-Inf, Inf).

Inference Layer

Influence functions (R/if_*.R): compute per-observation influence values for variance estimation. Use Gaussian kernel density estimation with bandwidth h = 0.79 * IQR * N^(-1/5) and numerical integration via integrate().

R/rescaled_bootstrap.R: variance estimation for complex survey designs. Two modes: - Stratified SRS: rescales deviations from stratum means - PSU-based complex designs: rescales weights by PSU selection counts - Default bootstrap replicate size: m_h = floor((n_h - 2)² / (n_h - 1))

Theoretical/Superpopulation

R/superpop_qri.R: computes the theoretical QRI for parametric distributions (not empirical data).

Documentation

All functions use roxygen2 inline comments. After editing any @param, @return, @export, or @importFrom, run devtools::document() to regenerate NAMESPACE and man/*.Rd files. Mathematical formulas are written in LaTeX via \deqn{}. References use \insertRef{} / \insertCite{} from Rdpack.

Key Dataset

data/synthouse.rda — a synthetic household survey (20,034 individuals, 10,099 households) mimicking Italian EU-SILC 2024. The primary variable of interest is eq_income (equivalized disposable income) with weight as the sampling weight. Geographic nesting: NUTS1 > NUTS2 > NUTS3 > municipality.

On this page

Developed by Silvia Scarpa.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.3.
